content_blocks.text:
Mercury, Saturn, Jupiter and Mars are his neutrals,
and the Moon is his enemy.

Sloka. 44. The Sun, Mars and the Moon are the
friends of Jupiter. His enemies are Venus and Mercury;
Saturn is neutral to him. Saturn and Mercury are the
friends of Venus. His enemies are the Sun and the
Moon. Jupiter and Mars are his neutrals.

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
Sloka. 44. The Sun, Mars and the Moon are the
friends of Jupiter. His enemies are Venus and Mercury;
Saturn is neutral to him. Saturn and Mercury are the
friends of Venus. His enemies are the Sun and the
Moon. Jupiter and Mars are his neutrals.

content_blocks.text:
Sloka. 45. The enemies of Saturn are the Sun, the
Moon and Mars. Jupiter is neutral towards him. Venus
and Mercury are his friends. The planets are to be
deemed exceedingly friendly or inimical by their being
friendly or inimical both naturally and for the time
being. Thus planet are divided into 5 classes, viz, (1)
मिष- Mithra, (2) अधित्रि Adhimithra, (3) शत्रु - Sathru,
(4) अधिशसु - Adhisathru and (5) सम- Sama.

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
Sloka. 45. The enemies of Saturn are the Sun, the
Moon and Mars. Jupiter is neutral towards him. Venus
and Mercury are his friends. The planets are to be
deemed exceedingly friendly or inimical by their being
friendly or inimical both naturally and for the time
being. Thus planet are divided into 5 classes, viz, (1)
मिष- Mithra, (2) अधित्रि Adhimithra, (3) शत्रु - Sathru,
(4) अधिशसु - Adhisathru and (5) सम- Sama.

content_blocks.text:
NOTES.
In order to find out the natural friendship or otherwise of
planets with respect to any one of the rest, the following
general rule is laid down by मलानाथ Satyacharya: Dis.,

सुहृदस्त्रिकोणभवनाहूहस्य सुतमे व्ययेऽथ धनभवने ।
स्वजने निधने धर्मे स्वोच्चे च भवन्ति नो शेषाः ॥

verse_data.sanskrit_text:
सुहृदस्त्रिकोणभवनाहूहस्य सुतमे व्ययेऽथ धनभवने ।
स्वजने निधने धर्मे स्वोच्चे च भवन्ति नो शेषाः ॥

content_blocks.text:
Planets are friendly to any given planet when they happen to
own the 5th, 12th, 2nd, 4th, 8th, and 9th houses counted from the
planet's Moolatrikona Rasi, or when they happen to own the
planet's exaltation Rasi. The rest are his enemies.

verse_data.translation:
Planets are friendly to any given planet when they happen to
own the 5th, 12th, 2nd, 4th, 8th, and 9th houses counted from the
planet's Moolatrikona Rasi, or when they happen to own the
planet's exaltation Rasi. The rest are his enemies.

content_blocks.text:
Place the planet in its Moolatrikona and mark the 2nd, 12th,
5th, 9th, the planet's exaltation Rasi, the 8th and the 10th. Planets
twice invited become friends; planets once invited become
acquaintances and planets that are uninvited become enemies. But
the Sun and the Moon become friends on a single invitation (as
each owns only a single sign in the Zodiac).
The Sun invites Mars twice, Jupiter twice, the Moon once and
Mercury once. Venus and Saturn are uninvited. Therefore Mars,

own the 5th, 12th, 2nd, 4th, 8th, and 9th houses counted from the
